intro.title: Sample Exam
intro.body: This sample exam was converted from the provided DOCX so WiseMock can be tested without AI features or a Groq API key.

sections.name: Section 1 - Conceptual Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: Questions focused on programming concepts, data structures, functions, loops, conditionals, and classes.

questions.title: What is the main reason to implement a function?
options: To reduce the number of errors
To make the program run automatically
To avoid code duplication and give the computation a clear purpose
To force the use of parameters

questions.title: When selecting a data structure, what is the main reason behind choosing a list?
options: The problem involves an ordered collection that may change over time

questions.title: What is the difference between print and return?
options: print displays information to the console, while return provides a value back to the caller
print can not be used after return
print can only display strings, while return can display any type

questions.title: Why are classes useful in program design?
options: They allow grouping related attributes (data) and behaviors (code)

questions.title: Which approach best supports organizing larger programs?
options: Writing code in a single long file
Splitting behavior into functions and classes

questions.title: When is a for loop more appropriate than a while loop?
options: When iterating over a known sequence
When repeating until a condition becomes false

questions.title: You want to store student names grouped by class. Which structure fits best?
options: A list of student names and classes
A dictionary mapping class names to lists of students
A tuple of lists with student name and class
A list of dictionaries with no keys

questions.title: You write a function that checks whether a given student passed a given course. What should the arguments be?
options: The student's score and the passing score
A list of all students' scores

questions.title: What is the purpose of an else statement in an if-else structure?
options: To repeat code until a condition becomes false
To run code when none of the preceding if/elif conditions are true
To check multiple conditions at once
To stop the program if a condition is False

questions.title: Which of the following makes a program easier to maintain and extend?
options: Writing as few lines of code as possible.
Grouping behavior into functions and classes.
Using only lists and no dictionaries.

sections.name: Section 2 - Code Output Questions
sections.instructions: Questions where the student must read code and identify the output or error.

questions.title:
What is the output of the following code?
pets = ["Tiki", "Baltas", "Sal"]
print(pets[-1])

What is the output of the following code?
schedule = {
    "Mon": ("Math", "Physics"),
    "Tue": ("History", "Art"),
    "Wed": ("Biology", "Chemistry")
}
print(schedule["Tue"][2])

What is the output of the following code?
def add_item(item, collection=None):
    if collection == []:
        return [item]
    collection.append(item)
    return collection
print(add_item("ABBA"))

What is the output of the following code?
first = "Lady"
last = "Gaga"
def full_name(first="Freddie", last="Mercury"):
    return f"{first} {last}"
print(full_name("Freddie", "Gibbs"))

What is the output of the following code?
class Task:
    def __init__(self, title, priority=0):
        self.title = title
        self.priority = priority
        self.state = "TODO"
    def toggle_state(self):
        if self.state == "TODO":
            self.state = "DONE"
        else:
            self.state = "TODO"
task1 = Task("Wash carpets")
task2 = Task("Vet appointment", 100)
task1.toggle_state()
task2.toggle_state()
task2.toggle_state()
print(f"{task1.priority}, {task1.state}")
print(f"{task2.priority}, {task2.state}")

sections.name: Section 3 - Fill in the Blank
sections.instructions: Questions where the student must choose the missing expression or statement.

questions.title: You are writing a program that, given an ordered list of books returned to a library, prints the title of the most recently returned book. As books are returned, they are added to the end of the list.
You are writing a program to check if anchovy is an ingredient of pizza. If so, the program should print OK, otherwise it should print Missing ingredient.
You are writing a program where you ask the user to input a number between 10 and 20. If their number is outside this range, you ask until a valid number is given.
You are writing a program that given a list of temperature readings and a threshold value, counts how many readings are above that value.

You are writing a function that, given a list of integers, returns the number of items that are greater than zero.
# Fill in the blank below

Consider the following function that loads an image from the file in path and resizes it to width x height pixels. You want to use this function to resize the image in file cover.jpeg to 600x600 pixels.

Consider the following class representing a player in a video game with a name, number of lives, and points. You want to add a method to use when the player is killed in the game, i.e., it loses a life.

sections.name: Section 4 - Correct Implementation
sections.instructions: Questions where the student must choose the implementation that correctly solves the problem.

questions.title: You are writing a program where you store client details indexed by the client's fiscal number. The details you want to store are the name, address, and list of past order numbers.
You are writing a program that takes a list of temperature readings and a threshold value and prints ALERT once if it finds at least one temperature above the threshold, or OK otherwise.
options:
temperatures = [21.5, 22.0, 22.4, 29.8, 30.1, 28.7]
threshold = 27
ok = True
for temp in temperatures:
    if temp > threshold:
        print("ALERT")
        break
 print("OK")

questions.title:
You want to implement a function that, given 1) a list of tuples of the form (color, size), 2) a color, and 3) a size, returns True if there is any tshirt in such a color and size, or False otherwise.
# A list with 3 tshirts. Two of them red and size S, one blue in size M.
tshirts = [("blue", "M"), ("red", "S"),  ("red", "S")]
# Check if there are any red shirts of size M
has_shirt(tshirts, "red", "M")
>>> False
# Check if there are any red shirts of size S
has_shirt(tshirts, "red", "S")
>>> True
options: This function is impossible to implement.

def transfer(self, player, points):
    player.points += points

def add_ingredient(self, name, amount):
    self.ingredients = amount

def add_ingredient(self, name, amount):
    self.ingredients[name] = amount
